verdict.label: RED · Stand down / roll DTE longer
verdict.desc: Overnight the U.S. struck Iran and President Trump declared the ceasefire “over,” with Tehran threatening 85 U.S. sites in the Gulf — oil jumped ~7% and equity futures are falling. Gap risk leapt to 68/100 (WARM, one notch from HOT) and P(≥1% gap) is 46% — above the 35% trigger — so a GREEN verdict is locked out. During an active, open-ended military conflict the overnight gap tail is unquantifiable and skewed down. Stand down on new credit spreads today, or push DTE out past the conflict window; this is not a premium-selling tape.

gap.heading: P(≥1% Overnight Gap to Thu Open)
gap.band_note: ABOVE the 35% danger line · GREEN locked out

focus.jbig: War headlines reset the tape · stand down
focus.jtag: U.S. struck Iran overnight, ceasefire declared over — oil spikes, futures fall
focus.jnote: The environment changed overnight — not for the better. The U.S. struck Iran in retaliation for attacks on tankers crossing the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump declared the interim ceasefire “over” and threatened further strikes, and Tehran said it had targeted 85 U.S. military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. Washington also revoked the waiver that let Iran sell crude. Oil jumped about 7% (WTI ~$74–75, Brent ~$80), the VIX popped to ~18, and equity futures are pointing lower. Gold actually slipped toward $4,050 as the dollar firmed. This is a live, open-ended conflict — exactly the kind of headline tape a Sniper does not sell premium into.
focus.notdown: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) and Defense (LMT, RTX, NOC) are the only groups catching a bid — oil supply fear lifts energy, conflict-demand lifts defense. But be honest: these are two-sided war trades, not clean Snipers. One de-escalation headline gaps them straight back down. Watch-only, not table-pounders.
focus.notup: Airlines & transports (DAL, UAL, AAL) and other fuel-sensitive cyclicals are pressured by the jet-fuel spike. But selling bear calls into an active-war, VIX-rising tape carries its own violent-squeeze risk on any peace headline — the disciplined answer is to sell nothing new today.
focus.why: Why RED? Composite 68 is deep in WARM, one point from HOT, and P(≥1% gap) at 46% is well above the 35% trigger — either one alone locks out GREEN. More important than the numbers: an active military exchange with an oil shock is an unscheduled, open-ended tail — worse than a known event like CPI because you cannot time or size around the next headline. Stand down on new credit spreads, or if you must be positioned, push DTE out past the conflict window and cut size hard. Cash is a position today.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~18.1, up ~12% and expanding off the strike headlines
Big events (next 1–3 days) — live war headlines, PEP earnings Thu, DAL Fri; CPI next Tue
World risk — active U.S.–Iran military exchange, ceasefire declared over, Gulf bases threatened
Volatility trend — front-month VIX spiking faster than 3-month; curve flattening toward stress
Commodities — oil supply shock, +7% on Hormuz risk; the biggest single swing factor

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news and energy is the day’s clearest bid — but this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation or ceasefire headline and crude gives back $5–7 in minutes, gapping your bull put the wrong way. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a RED day, don’t open new premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above the open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Conflict-demand lifts the defense primes, and structurally the story can run. But the same peace-headline risk cuts the other way: a ceasefire announcement gaps this group down hard. Selling a bull put here is a bet the war continues — not a probability edge a Sniper wants to underwrite. Confirm no earnings inside DTE (LMT/RTX report late-Jul).
setups.check_note: Same logic as energy — the bid is a war premium. Stand aside; revisit only once the tape settles.

setups.read: Jet fuel just jumped with crude, a direct headwind for airlines — the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a peace headline squeezes shorts violently, and DAL reports Fri 7/10 (earnings inside any normal DTE). A bear call into a VIX spike with a print pending is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Earnings + war squeeze risk = no. Do not carry an airline spread through Friday’s DAL print.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap this morning. Q2 season hasn’t started — PepsiCo (Thu) and Delta (Fri) are the first prints. Nothing has reported-and-gapped in the last 24–48h that qualifies. Check back once the calendar fills next week.
setups.check_note: Nothing to validate — no Type A candidate today.

setups.read: Risk-off gap-down morning — the exact tape where breakouts fail. The value/record-setters from late June (PNC, MNST, GL) are now well outside their 4-session windows, and no new clean high-volume ATH breakout is holding into an Iran-strike open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline-driven whipsaw
scenarios.sub: Choppy risk-off; the tape lurches on every Iran/oil headline, no clean trend, vol stays bid

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation
scenarios.sub: Iran hits a Gulf base or Hormuz shipping is disrupted; oil runs toward $85–90, equities gap down

scenarios.label: Good case — De-escalation
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire/backchannel headline; oil gives back the spike, a relief bounce in stocks, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran retaliation — Tehran named 85 U.S. sites in Bahrain and Kuwait; any actual strike on personnel or bases gaps the whole tape instantly. This is the number-one thing to watch.
Strait of Hormuz shipping — roughly a fifth of the world’s oil moves through it. Any attack or closure sends crude (and jet fuel) vertical — the week’s biggest tail.
Oil & the 10-year — WTI near $75 and climbing feeds inflation fear; the 10Y is already back to ~4.50%. A rate pop compounds pressure on stocks.
PEP (Thu) & DAL (Fri) — first Q2 prints; Delta is directly exposed to the fuel spike. Single-stock gap risk, not just the index.
CPI · Tue 7/14, PPI · Wed 7/15 — a hot inflation number stacked on top of an oil shock would be the worst-case macro combination.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is just starting — PepsiCo (Thu 7/9) and Delta (Fri 7/10) open it, then the big banks report ~July 14–15. With the market already on edge from the Iran shock, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Defensive staple — the market watches it for consumer-demand and pricing signals. Lower beta than the airlines, but a volume/guidance miss still gaps it. First read of the season.
rows.angle: Don’t hold a PEP spread through Thursday. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A into Friday.

rows.stock_sub: Delta · first airline of the season
rows.why: Consensus ~$1.48 EPS. The Iran-driven fuel spike now cuts the other way — higher jet fuel pressures margins and guidance. Directly in the crossfire of this week’s oil move.
rows.angle: Do not carry any airline spread through Friday. A miss-and-gap-down becomes a Type A bear-call setup next Monday.

rows.why: Real season opener — and they land the same days as CPI (7/14) and PPI (7/15), stacking macro and single-stock risk. Any bank spread must expire before its print.
rows.angle: Keep any financials spread to 7 days or fewer so it clears the earnings date — and mind the CPI/PPI overlap.

earnings.outro: Wed 7/8 – Fri 7/10: PepsiCo and Delta open the season; the rest is small-cap and off-cycle. It gets real next week: big banks ~Tue–Wed 7/14–15 alongside CPI/PPI, then the first mega-caps the week after.

rows.today: Risk-off · futures down on the strike
